title: -Contract: Siemens Industry, Inc. dba SIEMENS for Inspection, Testing, and Monitoring Services for the City’s Fire and Life Safety System Equipment

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and subsequent
amendments with Siemens Industry, Inc, for Inspection, Testing, Monitoring, and On-
Call Maintenance Services for Fire and Life Safety equipment installed in 23 City owned
buildings; in an amount not to exceed $350,000 for the initial contract period March 1,
2026, through February 28, 2029, and with the option to extend the contract term for
two additional years, for a total contract term of five years.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds are available in the adopted FY2026 Public Works Budget (Funds 011, 601, 627,
636, 672, and 673) for Fire and Life Safety services. The yearly cost for this contract is
estimated to be $115,000-$120,000. Funding for future years is subject to Council’s
approval of the proposed citywide budget for Fiscal Years 2027, 2028, and 2029.
sections.current_situation:
The Public Works Department is responsible for maintaining the Fire and Life Safety
equipment deployed throughout 23 different City buildings to ensure the equipment
functions properly at all times and to maintain compliance with National Fire Protection
Association (NFPA) guidelines and the California Fire Code. This includes semi-annual
and annual inspection and testing of the Fire and Life Safety equipment, quarterly
inspection and testing of sprinkler systems, Five Year Sprinkler System Certifications,
and 24 hour per day monitoring of the City’s Fire Alarm systems.
The current contract for Inspection, Testing, and Monitoring of the City’s Fire and Life
Safety systems expires on March 31, 2026. Authorization to execute a new Fire and
Life Safety contract, to start March 1, 2026, ensures continued City compliance with
NFPA guidelines and the State Fire Code.
Maintaining this critical safety infrastructure and around-the-clock monitoring of Fire and
Life Safety systems is in alignment with the City’s Strategic Plan Priority of advancing

sections.background:
The City issued Request for Proposal Specification No. 25-11727-C for Fire and Life
Safety System Inspection, Testing, and Monitoring services to support the Public Works
Facilities and Building Maintenance Division, on September 4, 2025, and received three
qualified responsive proposals. A selection panel comprised of Department subject
matter experts evaluated the proposals based on the following criteria: Project
Approach (30%), Expertise and References (20%), Cost (30%), and Background (20%).
Siemens was selected as the most responsive, qualified, and cost-effective vendor, able
to service the full range of manufacturer’s equipment and to meet the comprehensive
needs outlined in the RFP Scope of Services for all 23 City service locations.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental sustainability or climate impacts associated with
the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Public Works does not have the personnel or specialized equipment necessary to
monitor the City’s Fire Alarm systems 24 hours a day or the expertise to inspect, test
and certify the other components of the City’s Fire and Life Safety systems. The existing
contract for Fire and Life System Inspection and Testing is due to expire March 31,
2026. Executing a new contract ensures that critical City safety infrastructure is well
maintained and functions properly and that the City continues to follow local and state
fire safety regulations.
sections.alternatives:
Public Works does not currently have staff with the experience and skills necessary to
monitor, service, and certify the City’s Fire and Life Safety infrastructure. As a result,
contracting with an outside vendor is a necessity.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Public Works Operations Manager, (510)981-6629

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 5
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: SIEMENS INDUSTRIES, INC. FOR INSPECTION, TESTING, AND
MONITORING OF THE CITY’S FIRE AND LIFE SAFETY SYSTEM
INFRASTRUCTURE
WHEREAS, the City’s buildings require monitoring and testing of installed Fire and Life
Safety Systems; and
WHEREAS, the City does not have the specialized training or staff to perform this work;
and
WHEREAS, the City released RFP Specification No. 2511727-C on September 4, 2025;
and
WHEREAS, the City received three qualified, responsive proposals and a selection
panel comprised of subject matter experts from the Public Works Operations Divisions
evaluated the proposals; and
WHEREAS, Siemens Industries, Inc. was selected as the most responsive and qualified
vendor; and
WHEREAS, Inspection, Testing, and Monitoring to maintain the functional integrity of
the City’s Fire and Life Safety System infrastructure in City facilities is essential to the
safety of City staff and to the public and ensures continued compliance with National
Fire Protection Association (NFPA) guidelines and the California Fire Code; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the Fiscal Year 2026 adopted budget in Funds 011,
601, 627, 672, and 673. Funding for future years is subject to Council’s approval for the
proposed citywide budget for Fiscal Years 2027, 2028, and 2029.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and subsequent amendments with
Siemens Industry, Inc, for Inspection, Testing, Monitoring, and On-Call Maintenance
Services for the Fire and Life Safety equipment installed in 23 City owned buildings; in
an amount not to exceed $350,000 for the initial contract period March 1, 2026, through
February 28, 2029, and with the option to extend the contract term for two additional
years, for a total contract term of five years.
